description: Outside a theater at night, an illuminated marquee reads "CIRCLE IN THE SQUARE," and people walk toward the entrance. Inside, Ross and Chandler walk down an aisle through a seated audience. An off-screen voice says, "You."

Ross and Chandler sit in the theater seats. Chandler says, "Okay, she is the star of the play, and she is my girlfriend. I get to have sex with the star of the play," smiling widely, as audience laughter is heard.
Chandler continues smiling while Ross looks at him.
Ross turns toward Chandler and says softly, "People can hear you."
Chandler laughs and says loudly, "I know," followed by audience laughter.
The ambient lighting dims. Audience members' chatter fades to silence. Ross and Chandler turn their attention forward.
An off-screen voice says, "It's."
Ross and Chandler keep their gaze directed forward toward the off-screen stage.
Chandler leans slightly toward Ross and whispers, "Wow, she looks great, doesn't she?" Ross replies softly, "It hi."
Chandler looks straight ahead.
Ross looks forward and says softly, "That is one good looking man." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler stares forward with his lips slightly parted.
Still staring forward, Chandler whispers, "Is it just me, or can you actually see his ABS through his overcoat?" Audience laughter plays.
Ross and Chandler keep their eyes focused ahead.
An off-screen male voice asks, "So you've been doing this long?"
An off-screen female voice replies, "No, you're my first. Put the money on the table." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler's eyes widen, and he swallows visibly.
The off-screen female voice exhales audibly and says, "Oh, yeah."
Chandler's mouth opens slightly as he stares forward.
The off-screen female voice moans, saying, "OOH, that's nice." Chandler's eyebrows draw together.
Chandler closes his eyes briefly, shifts in his seat, and lowers his head amid audience laughter.
Ross looks at Chandler and says softly, "Dude,"
Chandler keeps his eyes locked forward, his mouth slightly open, while Ross looks at him.
Ross asks, "Is that an expensive blouse?"
An off-screen male voice says, "If you want it to be."
The sound of tearing fabric is audible off-screen, followed by audience laughter. Chandler flinches, closing his eyes tightly and turning his head slightly away. Ross looks down into his lap.
Ross holds out his hand toward Chandler, holding a small object, and says, "Here's your girlfriend's button," followed by audience laughter.
Chandler stares at the button in Ross's hand, his mouth remaining open, and does not reach for it.
The setting changes to the exterior of an apartment building at night, with illuminated windows.
Inside an apartment living room, several cardboard boxes rest on the floor. Phoebe stands holding a framed picture, while Monica organizes clothes on top of a box.
Phoebe holds the frame and asks, "Oh, hey, mom, do you still have, like, your old blouses and dresses from high school?"
Monica turns toward Phoebe and replies, "Yeah, I think I have some around here somewhere. Why?"
Phoebe answers, "Well, it's just maternity clothes are so expensive," as audience laughter plays.
Monica turns her attention back to the clothes. Rachel opens the front door and walks in, carrying multiple purses over her arm.
Monica speaks to Rachel as Rachel walks in, "Hey, Rach, I've made a pile of your stuff on this side of the room. If you could just"
Rachel tosses her purses onto a nearby box.
Monica watches Rachel and finishes her sentence, saying, "throw your purses at it," followed by audience laughter.
Rachel sits heavily on the sofa.
Rachel leans back with a sigh and says, "Bloomingdale's eliminated my department."
Monica gasps, her eyes widening, and asks, "Oh, my God. Are you out of a job?"
Rachel waves her hand dismissively and says, "No, but they stuck me in Personal Shopping, which is just a huge step down. Personal"
Phoebe looks at Rachel and asks, "Shopping? What is that, like, where you walk around with snooty rich people and tell them what to buy?"
Rachel nods slowly, her lips pressed into a tight line.
Phoebe smiles broadly and says, "That sounds great," amid audience laughter.
Rachel stares at Phoebe. Joey opens the front door and enters the apartment.
Joey says, "Hey," as he walks toward the kitchen area.
Joey opens the refrigerator door and peers inside.
Monica says, "Excuse me."
Monica points her finger toward Joey and says, "We switched apartments."
Monica continues pointing, saying, "You can't eat our food anymore."
Monica lowers her hand and says, "That gravy train has ended his gravy," followed by audience laughter.
Joey turns toward Monica, holding a fried chicken leg.
Joey points the chicken leg at Monica and says, "If you have the big apartment, then you have to deal with people coming over all the time. That fridge has got to be"
Joey gestures toward the door with the chicken leg and finishes, "stocked. Okay, that's your apartment now," accompanied by audience laughter.
Monica stares at Joey. Joey turns around, opens an upper cabinet above the counter, and climbs up onto the counter.
Monica asks, "What are you doing?" Joey answers while looking into the high cabinet, "I think I left a donut up here." Audience laughter plays.
The setting changes to a city street at night, with traffic moving. Inside a coffee shop, Phoebe and Joey sit on a large sofa. Ross and Chandler walk toward them.
Ross says, "Hey."
Phoebe looks up from a book she is holding.
Phoebe asks, "Oh. How was Kathy's place?"
Ross sits on the arm of the sofa, gesturing toward Chandler, and says, "Well, Kathy gets half naked and simulates sex with a real good looking"
Ross finishes, "guy," as Chandler steps forward.
Chandler gestures with his hands and says loudly, "It's like somebody literally wrote down my worst nightmare and then charged me $32 to see it," followed by audience laughter.
Phoebe looks at Chandler.
Phoebe points a finger toward Chandler and says, "That's a good idea for a business," as audience laughter plays.
Chandler looks at Phoebe, his lips pressed together.
Chandler begins pacing and says, "I'm totally screwed, okay? They're going to be hot and heavy on stage every night, and then they're going to go to their little cast"
Chandler stops pacing, gesturing with one hand, and continues, "parties, and he's going to try to undermine me. He'll be like, So where's your boyfriend? What's his name?"
Chandler uses a deeper voice to say, "Chester."
Chandler pauses, gesturing with one hand.
Chandler says, "And she'll no, no, it's Chandler. And he'll go, whatever," accompanied by audience laughter.
Chandler throws his arms out to his sides and drops them, keeping his eyes fixed on Phoebe and Joey, as audience laughter continues.
In a coffee shop, Chandler stands behind a couch where Ross, Joey, and Phoebe are seated. Chandler looks down and then raises his head.
An uncertain speaker says, "That is a good".
Chandler gestures with his hands, looking around at the others.
Joey, seated next to Ross, speaks to Chandler. "All right, look, look. What am I going to do, Chandler? Look, they're actors."
Chandler sits down on the arm of a nearby chair as Joey continues, "They're there to do a job. Just because they work"
Joey gestures with his hands and continues, "together doesn't mean they're going to get together. I mean, just because it happened with, like, Susan Sarandon and Tim"
Joey finishes, "Robbins doesn't mean it's going to happen with, oh, Alec Baldwin and Kim Basinger." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey points at Chandler and says, "Oh, Tom Cruise and Nicole Kidman."
Chandler turns his head toward Phoebe.
Phoebe, sitting in an armchair, says, "Okay, Mel Gibson and Clint Eastwood."
Ross and Joey turn their heads toward Phoebe.
Ross gestures to Phoebe and says, "They're not a couple." Phoebe nods and responds, "Oh, okay. I get the game now." Audience laughter sounds.
Chandler leans forward slightly.
Chandler gestures with both hands and says, "All these people are movies together. It's much different with plays."
Chandler points to his chest and then to Joey, saying, "You guys were just in acting class together, and she slept with you."
Joey says, "Acting class is a lot more like a movie than a play."
Chandler holds his arms out to his sides.
Joey leans forward and asks, "Okay, look, let me ask you a question. When they were doing it on stage, was it, like, really hot?"
Chandler responds, "Oh, yeah," keeping his gaze lowered.
Joey nods.
Joey gestures with his hands and says, "Well, okay, so then you're fine. Look, the rule is, when two actors are actually doing it off stage then"
Joey continues, "all the sexual tension between them is gone."
Joey points at Chandler and says, "Okay? So as long as it's hot on stage, you got nothing to worry about. It's when the heat goes away, that's when you're in trouble."
Chandler's eyebrows remain drawn together.
Chandler says, "Really?" Joey turns to Ross and Phoebe, asking, "Look, you guys have been to every play I've ever been in. Have I ever had chemistry on stage?"
Ross, Chandler, and Phoebe answer in unison, "No."
Chandler looks straight ahead as audience laughter sounds. The setting changes to a city skyline at dusk, with buildings silhouetted against an orange sky.

In an apartment kitchen, Chandler sits at an island, eating. Ross sits opposite him, also eating. Joey stands at the counter in the background. Chandler asks, "So, man, are you going to go see the play with me tonight?"
Ross gestures with his hands and replies, "You know what? I don't know how comfortable I am going to see how hot the sex is between some guy and your girlfriend."
Chandler begins to answer, "Yeah, I know, but"
Ross points his fork at Chandler and says, "No, I'm there."
Monica walks through the front door into the apartment.
Joey turns from the stove and asks, "Hey, man, want some pancakes?"
Monica asks, "You made pancakes?"
Joey replies, "Yes. Grab a plate."
Monica walks to the island, picks up a plate, and holds it out.
Joey, holding a pan, points with his free hand and says, "No, stay right there."
Joey gestures with the pan. Monica takes a small step forward, keeping her plate extended.
Chandler looks toward Monica and says, "Getting closer."
Monica takes another step forward, her lips turning up slightly.
Chandler gestures over the food on the island and says, "Okay, but don't worry, because we also have cereals, muffins waffles and jams, jellies and marmalades. Which I'm"
Chandler continues, "fairly certain of the same thing, by by the way. But please eat, because Joey opened everything." Phoebe, standing near the fridge, tosses a piece of food into her mouth.
Joey turns from the stove, holding a spatula, and looks at Chandler.
Joey points the spatula toward the living area and says, "Listen, also, we're watching the game here Saturday night if people want to come."
Ross points and says, "Oh, yeah." Monica's eyes widen as she says, "Oh, I was thinking about having people over for the game." Joey asks, "Yeah? Who's playing?"
Monica looks back and forth, her eyebrows drawn together.
Monica answers, "The players."
Joey turns back to the stove. Ross places his hands on Monica's shoulders.
Ross says, "Somebody seems to be missing being the hostess."
Monica shakes her head, her lips turning up slightly.
Monica shrugs off Ross's hands, gesturing, and says, "Please. It's a relief, is what it is. It's what it is."
Joey calls out, turning from the stove.
Joey says, "All right, beeb, stick out your plate." Phoebe holds out her plate.
Joey tosses a pancake from the pan. The pancake flies through the air, and Phoebe catches it on her plate, giving a thumbs-up.

The setting changes to a city street with traffic, including yellow taxis and a red scooter passing a building. The scene then changes to an office interior where Rachel is pacing and talking on a cell phone.
Rachel paces and says into her phone, "Monica, I'm quitting."
Rachel continues pacing, gesturing with her free hand, and says, "I just helped an 81 year old woman put on a thong and she didn't even buy it."
Rachel turns and walks back across the room.
Rachel says, "That's telling you I'm quitting. That's it. I'm talking to my boss right now. Yes, I am. Yes, I am. Yes, I"
Rachel continues speaking at a faster pace, "am. Yes, I am. Yes, I am. Yes, I am."
Rachel says, "Okay, bye. Call me when you get this message," and lowers the phone.
Mr. Walton enters the office holding a jacket on a hanger.
Rachel turns toward him and says, "Oh, Mr."
Mr. Walton steps further into the room.
Rachel clasps her hands together and says, "Walton, I really need to talk to you." Mr. Walton replies, "In a moment, please."
Mr. Walton hands the jacket to Rachel and says, "I'm in the middle of a task, and you have a"
Rachel holds the jacket, looking at Mr. Walton as he steps past her.
In a brightly lit office, Rachel stands near the doorway with her hands clasped. Joshua steps into the room.
Rachel says, "Hi."
Joshua approaches, extends his hand, and they shake hands. He says, "Hi. I'm Joshua."
Rachel smiles and replies, "Hi, I'm Rachel Green."
Rachel asks, "What can I do for you, Joshua?"
Joshua gestures with his hands and replies, "Well, I need a whole new wardrobe. My wife well, my ex wife"
Rachel presses a hand to her chest and says, "Oh, I'm so sorry."
Rachel turns away, quickly checks her reflection in a wall mirror, straightens her clothing, and turns back to face Joshua.
Joshua says, "Anyway, she burned all of my clothes." Rachel touches her hair.
Joshua gestures down toward his clothing and says, "So I got away with two things this suit and what turned out to be a skirt."
Rachel smiles at Joshua.
Rachel says, "That's a great suit."
Joshua pulls the lapels of his jacket and replies, "Yeah, but it wasn't much fun dropping it off of the dry cleaners in the skirt."
Rachel laughs loudly. Joshua smiles.
Joshua gestures with both hands and says, "So I need everything down to underwear. So if you're willing, I'm all yours."
Rachel crosses her arms and smiles.
Rachel says, "Okay."
An older man steps up behind Rachel.
The older man begins, "Rachel, you needed to speak to". Rachel quickly turns, points at him, and interrupts, "No, no, that wasn't me." The man turns and walks away.
Rachel turns back to Joshua.
Rachel gestures with her hands and says, "Well, we should get started. Let me show you my underwear."
Joshua raises his eyebrows. Rachel quickly closes her eyes and lowers her head.
Rachel claps her hands together and quickly adds, "The selection of underwear that we carry."
Rachel gestures toward the doorway and walks in that direction. Joshua follows her.
They both exit the room. The scene changes to the illuminated exterior of a building with a sign reading "CIRCLE IN THE SQUARE" at night. People walk on the sidewalk.
The scene changes to a theater lobby. Ross sits on a bench. Chandler stands nearby, holding a rolled-up program.
Chandler paces slightly, repeatedly pointing the rolled-up program at Ross, who watches him.
Chandler stops pacing and says, "Now, you know what this means."
Chandler points the program and says, "Joey told us what this means."
Ross stands up, gesturing with his hands, and replies, "All right, let's not jump to any conclusions, all right? There was some sexual chemistry between them. Come"
Chandler grimaces, gestures with his hands, and says, "on. It was like cousins having sex up there."
Kathy walks through a curtained doorway in the background. Ross notices her and pats Chandler's arm.
Ross points toward Kathy and tells Chandler, "Here she comes. Don't say a word, okay? Just be cool."
Ross points directly at Chandler and adds, "Don't be you."
Kathy walks toward them, smiling.
Kathy stops in front of them and says, "Hey, you guys."
Kathy hugs Chandler and says, "Oh, thank you so much for coming again."
Kathy looks at Chandler and asks, "Did you like it tonight?"
Chandler smiles with his lips pressed together and answers, "Absolutely."
Kathy smiles and rubs her hands together.
Kathy laughs and asks, "Wasn't Nick funny when he couldn't get his match lit." Chandler forces a laugh.
Kathy stops laughing, looks down at her hands, and adjusts a ring on her finger.
Kathy clasps her hands together and asks, "It's a good play, isn't it?"
Chandler looks at her and nods.
Chandler replies, "I love the play."
Chandler gestures with his hand and continues, "Yeah, you were great. And Nick ditto."
Chandler clasps his hands in front of him.
Chandler states, "Clearly I'm having sex with him."
Kathy's smile drops, and she stares at Chandler. Ross shifts his weight and looks down.
Kathy says, "Okay,"
Kathy turns her head slightly, keeping her eyes fixed on Chandler.
Kathy repeats, "Clearly I'm having sex with him." Chandler gestures with his hands, "Oh, come on. It was so obvious there was no chemistry between you two."
Kathy points at herself, then at Chandler, asking, "Okay, let me just get this straight. You're accusing me of cheating on you and you're insulting my performance?"
Chandler gestures outward and says, "I can see how this could happen."
Chandler gestures with both hands as he says, "You're up there every night, naked, touching, kissing, acting general."
Kathy responds, "This is my job. I'm"
Kathy touches her chest, adding, "playing a part in a play."
Kathy asks, "How can you not trust me?"
Chandler shrugs and says, "Well, you can understand, given how we started."
Kathy steps back, her eyes widening, and says, "Oh, wow. I can't believe you're throwing that in my face."
Chandler gestures and says, "Well, that is what happened. And I don't even see you denying this."
Kathy points at him and says, "I'll tell you what, Chandler. Why don't you call me when you grow up?"
Kathy turns and walks away. Chandler calls after her, "Yeah, well, don't expect that to happen anytime soon."
Chandler stands still, his hands in his pockets, watching Kathy walk away.
The scene changes to an apartment living room. Rachel opens the front door and walks in.

Rachel smiles broadly and says, "I have the best job in the entire world. The most adorable guy came over today and I got to dress him up all day." Joey, holding a slice of pizza, and Ross look at her. Phoebe sits in a nearby chair.
Rachel continues walking further into the apartment, still smiling.
In an apartment living room, Rachel stands wearing a black coat and a purple scarf. Ross sits on the arm of a nearby chair holding a slice of pizza.
Joey speaks from off-screen, "Rachel has a new doll."
Rachel looks down at her scarf as audience laughter plays.
Rachel says, "I wish he was a doll," while pulling the ends of her scarf.

Rachel grips the ends of her scarf and brings her fists together, saying, "Then I could get a Rachel doll and I'd bum them together and make kissing noises." Ross watches her as audience laughter is heard.
Rachel takes off her scarf and begins removing her coat while Ross takes a bite of his pizza.
Rachel continues removing her coat and says, "Oh, Andy's got the most beautiful name. I never"
Rachel tosses her coat onto a chair, then turns back and throws her arms down. She mouths a word, then raises one arm high to audience laughter.
With her arm raised, Rachel says, "Joshua."
Rachel lowers her arms. Ross turns his head toward her.
Ross says, "Hello," prompting audience laughter.
Rachel steps toward Ross.
Rachel places her hands on Ross's shoulders from behind and smiles, saying, "Hi," over audience laughter.
Rachel walks away. Ross continues eating his pizza. Phoebe is visible sitting in an armchair, holding a small plate.
Joey enters the room sniffing the air. "OOH. What do I smell?" he asks. Ross replies, "I don't know. It smells good." Audience laughter plays.
Joey turns and walks toward a closed door across the room.
Monica opens the door and steps into the apartment holding a plate of food.
Monica smiles and says, "Fresh cookies."
Monica steps further into the room.
Monica walks toward the kitchen area and says, "Hot from the oven."
Holding the plate out to Joey and Ross, Monica says, "Please have some."
Joey and Ross take cookies from the plate. Monica says, "Oh, it's been fiddling around in here making delicious treats for everyone."
Joey picks up a magazine from a nearby box and asks, "It's a new playboy." Monica replies, "Yeah,"
"Just something I picked up," Monica continues with a smile.
Ross eats his cookie and says, "Cookies and porn," to audience laughter.
Ross points at Monica and says, "You're the best mom ever," followed by audience laughter.
The group gathers around the kitchen table. Ross, Joey, and Rachel stand while Phoebe sits, all holding cookies. Joey looks through the magazine.
Rachel suddenly laughs loudly. Phoebe turns her head to look at her.
Phoebe asks, "What?"
Rachel waves her hand dismissively.
Rachel gestures with her hand and says, "Joshua said about V necks, but you had to be there."
Ross looks at Rachel and points his finger.
"How does Jason look in a V neck?" Ross asks. "It's Joshua," Rachel replies, over audience laughter.
Ross gestures with his hand and says, "Oh, whatever."
Joey closes the magazine. He and Ross turn and begin walking toward the door.
Phoebe stands up from her chair and says, "Wait, this isn't takeout."
Monica steps forward with her arms spread wide and says, "Hey, eat and run. No, wait. Please don't go," as audience laughter plays.
Monica looks at Phoebe and says, "I've got porn for you too," to audience laughter.
Phoebe looks at Monica.
Phoebe turns to leave and says, "Yeah, I don't need it," prompting audience laughter.
Phoebe begins walking toward the door.
Monica gestures openly and says, "People are supposed to want to hang out here." Rachel asks, "Why? Honey, what is the big deal?"
Monica presses a hand to her chest and says, "I'm the hostess."
Monica lowers her hands.
Monica gestures emphatically while speaking, "Not those guys. I'm always the hostess. Even when I was little."
Monica continues, "I mean, the girls brought their dollies to my"
"Tea party. I served the best air," Monica finishes to audience laughter.
Rachel looks at Monica without responding as the screen turns black.
The screen remains black until the segment ends.
